title: Does Gemtesa cause hair loss?
content:
It’s not likely. Hair loss was not reported as a side effect in Gemtesa’s studies.

There may be another reason you are experiencing hair loss while taking Gemtesa. Understanding the root cause of hair loss can help you and your doctor determine the best way to manage this condition.

Hair loss may be caused by:

Reflection time: Have you noticed patterns when hair loss tends to feel more noticeable?

The best way to slow or stop hair loss is to address the underlying cause. In some cases, hair loss may be temporary. For example, hair loss caused by pregnancy may stop or slow down after the birth of the child.

Read more: Best Hair Growth Products in 2026

If you experience hair loss while taking Gemtesa, talk with your doctor. They can recommend the best way to manage hair loss. 

Note: Before starting any new medications or supplements, be sure to talk with your doctor or pharmacist to make sure they are safe for you.

title: When do you stop taking Aricept?
content:
Deciding when to stop treatment with Aricept requires a careful evaluation of how the drug is working for you, whether you are experiencing bothersome side effects, or a shift toward palliative comfort.

Aricept can be an important tool for managing dementia related to Alzheimer’s symptoms. There comes a point in the disease’s progression where the medication may no longer provide a meaningful benefit. Understanding how to transition off the drug safely is key to avoiding sudden withdrawal symptoms or a sharp decline in functioning.

Your doctor may recommend stopping Aricept treatment for a variety of reasons. Some examples include:

Don’t stop taking Aricept without first talking with your doctor. They will want to discuss your treatment options and how to safely switch medications.

Quick note: People reading this article may be caregivers for someone taking Aricept, and that can be overwhelming. Take a moment to take a few breaths before continuing to read this article. If helpful, you can bookmark this page to refer back to during a doctor’s appointment.

If your doctor decides that stopping Aricept is the best for your treatment, they will likely recommend gradually tapering the dose. It is best not to stop Aricept abruptly. This may lead to withdrawal symptoms like delirium, confusion, or temporary worsening in thinking and function.

Your doctor will determine the best timeline for tapering you off of Aricept and possibly starting a new medication. They will monitor you closely for changes in thinking, behavior, or daily function.

title: Does Rinvoq cause acne?
content:
Yes, acne is a documented common side effect of Rinvoq.

In clinical trials: 

Acne often occurs in people taking Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors, such as Rinvoq. The chances of this side effect seem to depend on the dose and the condition being treated. 

Higher doses of Rinvoq are more likely to cause acne and other skin problems, and acne seems to be a more common side effect in people using the drug for atopic dermatitis. 

Acne caused by Rinvoq typically appears on the face, forehead, chin, and upper body. 

Most reported cases of acne from Rinvoq are mild to moderate, and often respond to common acne treatments. 

Researchers do not yet fully understand why upadacitinib, the active ingredient in Rinvoq, can trigger acne. 

One theory is that blocking JAK signaling changes how skin cells, oil glands, and hair follicles work, creating conditions that make breakouts more likely. 

The drug also suppresses the immune system, making it harder for the body to fight infections.

More research is still needed to confirm the exact mechanism by which Rinvoq affects the skin.

Optum Perks is owned by RVO Health. By clicking on this link, we may receive a commission. Learn more.

Because Rinvoq is an immunosuppressant that affects how your immune system works, it may cause other side effects, including:

Although rare, Rinvoq may also increase the risk of:

If you are developing acne after starting on Rinvoq:

title: Should I keep my Wegovy pen in the fridge?
content:
The storage guidelines for Wegovy depend on the type of pen you have and where you live. 

The ideal place to store Wegovy pens and syringes is in the fridge. However, the rules for storing the medication at room temperature differ depending on whether you’re using the multidose pens, which are common outside the United States, or the single-dose pens or syringes, which are used in the U.S.

This article explains how to store Wegovy, what to do with it when you travel, and how to tell whether it’s safe to use. 

Different types of Wegovy pens and syringes are available in different countries, so the storage guidelines also differ. 

In the U.S., Wegovy pens and syringes contain one dose and must be discarded after use. In most other countries, Wegovy is supplied in FlexTouch pens containing four doses, so you don’t discard them until you’ve used the fourth dose. The 7.2 mg dose is an exception, as that is supplied in a single-use pen or syringe in all countries.

The table below summarizes the Wegovy storage guidelines. The following sections explain them in more detail.

Ideal storage

fridge (36°F to 46°F/ 2°C to 8°C)

fridge (36°F to 46°F/ 2°C to 8°C)

fridge (36°F to 46°F/ 2°C to 8°C)

Room temperature limit (up to 86ºF/ 30ºC)

28 days (before cap removal)

6 weeks (before or during use)

28 days

After first use

discard immediately

can store for 6 weeks

discard immediately

The best place to store Wegovy prefilled single-dose pens or syringes is in the refrigerator. The temperature should stay between 36ºF and 46ºF (2ºC to 8ºC). This temperature helps keep the medication stable so it works effectively.

When you put your pens in the fridge, try to keep them in their original box. The box is designed to protect the medication from light, which can break down the ingredients and make them less effective.

If necessary, an unopened single-use Wegovy pen or syringe can be stored at room temperature (below 86ºF/ 30ºC) for up to 28 days, provided the cap has not been removed.

As soon as you remove the cap, give yourself the injection, then discard the pen.

The ideal way to store FlexTouch pens is in the fridge between 36ºF and 46ºF (2ºC to 8ºC).

If necessary, you can keep most Wegovy FlexTouch pens (0.25 mg to 2.4 mg) at room temperature (below 86ºF/ 30ºC) for up to 6 weeks. You can also take the pen out of the fridge and leave it at room temperature for up to 6 weeks.

If possible, refrigerate your Wegovy pen when you first get it and return it to the fridge right after each injection. Make sure you’ve removed the needle, replaced the cap, and put it back in its box.

Once you’ve had your last dose, dispose of the pen in a sharps container.

The 7.2 mg dose comes in a single-use pen, which, ideally, should be stored in the fridge. If necessary, it can be stored at room temperature for up to 28 days and then discarded after use.

To keep Wegovy stable, avoid doing the following:

Before every injection, check your medication in the window on the side of the device. Follow this guidance to determine whether your medication is safe or unsafe to use:

If your pen or syringe shows signs that it’s not safe to use, do not use it and contact Wegovy’s Patient Customer Care Center at 1-833-934-6891 for a replacement. 

Yes, you can still use it as long as the room didn’t get hotter than 86°F (30 ºC) overnight. Once it’s been at room temperature, you must use it within 28 days.

Yes, you can still use it as long as the room didn’t get hotter than 86°F (30 ºC) overnight. Once it’s been at room temperature, you must use it within 28 days.

If your Wegovy pen freezes, throw it away. Freezing changes the medication and can make it unsafe or ineffective.

If your Wegovy pen freezes, throw it away. Freezing changes the medication and can make it unsafe or ineffective.

Yes, you can return Wegovy to the fridge, but you still have to use it within 28 days from the time it warmed up to room temperature.

Yes, you can return Wegovy to the fridge, but you still have to use it within 28 days from the time it warmed up to room temperature.

Yes, you can inject cold Wegovy. However, if you find that injecting cold medication is uncomfortable, you can let it sit out for a short time to reach room temperature. Never try to warm it up using a microwave, hot water, or any other heat source, as this will damage the medication.

Yes, you can inject cold Wegovy. However, if you find that injecting cold medication is uncomfortable, you can let it sit out for a short time to reach room temperature. Never try to warm it up using a microwave, hot water, or any other heat source, as this will damage the medication.

Store Wegovy pills at room temperature (68°F to 77°F/ 20ºC to 25ºC) in a dry place. Keep them in their original bottle with the lid on.

Store Wegovy pills at room temperature (68°F to 77°F/ 20ºC to 25ºC) in a dry place. Keep them in their original bottle with the lid on.

To keep Wegovy working its best, store your injection pens or syringes in the fridge and keep them inside their original box to block out light.

In the U.S., you can keep Wegovy at room temperature for up to 28 days if a fridge is not available. 

Outside of the U.S, you can store most Wegovy pens at room temperature for up to 6 weeks. Regardless of where you live, you must never allow Wegovy to freeze or get hotter than 86ºF (30ºC).

title: How long does it take for Zepbound to start working?
content:
While you may notice a decrease in appetite and fewer food cravings within the first few days to a week of taking Zepbound, the most significant weight loss typically begins after your first 4 weeks. Although you may see the scale move slightly early on, your body needs time to adjust to the medication during the initial low-dose starting phase.

When you start Zepbound, your doctor follows a specific dosing schedule to help your body adapt and to minimize side effects. Here is what you can generally expect:

Note: The maximum dosage of Zepbound is 15 mg once per week. 

To learn more about what to expect at different dosages, read this Zepbound dosage guide.

Many people lose 10 to 15 pounds on a GLP-1 before they see any visible changes in the mirror. Why does the body often burn through “invisible” visceral fat around the organs before it tackles the visible, subcutaneous fat?

“Visceral fat is more sensitive to fat-burning hormones like adrenaline and breaks down quickly. The fat under your skin (subcutaneous) actually has receptors that slow down fat burning, meaning it actively resists the process. This is why visible changes take longer, even when the scale is moving.”

— Olivia Taylor, PharmD, BCPS

» Read next: How much weight can you lose on Zepbound?

Quotes represent the opinions of our medical experts. All content is strictly informational and should not be considered medical advice.

If you don’t see immediate results on the scale, it’s important to be patient. The initial 2.5 mg dose is intended to ease your body into the medication, not to cause significant weight loss right away.

Additionally, Zepbound is not a standalone cure. Its effectiveness relies heavily on being paired with a reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity. Individual factors, such as your starting weight, metabolism, and underlying medical conditions, may also influence how quickly you lose weight.

For more tips on managing your weight loss journey, take a look at Medical News Today’s weight management hub.

Talk with a healthcare professional if you have been on a higher maintenance dose of Zepbound for several months without seeing results, or if severe side effects are preventing you from increasing your dosage. To learn more about managing side effects, read this article on Zepbound’s side effects.

title: Redemplo 101: Everything you need to know
content:
Learn more about what Redemplo is used for, how to give yourself the injection, and what to expect as you start treatment.

Redemplo is approved by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to lower severely high triglyceride levels in adults with familial chylomicronemia syndrome (FCS). It is used in combination with a low fat diet.

FCS is a rare inherited genetic condition that affects fat metabolism. It affects the body’s ability to produce lipoprotein lipase (LPL), an enzyme crucial for breaking down triglycerides.

The recommended dosage of Redemplo is 25 milligrams (mg) given once every 3 months.

Redemplo is available as a solution inside a single-dose prefilled syringe. It comes in one strength: 25 mg/0.5 milliliters (mL).

Redemplo is given as a subcutaneous injection. It can be injected into the front of the thigh or abdomen. A caregiver or healthcare professional can also inject it into the back of your upper arm.

Optum Perks is owned by RVO Health. By clicking on this link, we may receive a commission. Learn more.

More common side effects reported with Redemplo include:

Other possible side effects of Redemplo include:

If you experience side effects that feel life threatening, call 911 or your local emergency right away.

These are not all the possible side effects of Redemplo. Talk with your doctor or pharmacist for more information. You can also refer to the drug’s prescribing information.

Experiencing side effects? After the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approves a drug, it tracks and reviews its side effects. If you develop a side effect while taking this medication and want to report it to the FDA, you can visit MedWatch or call 800-FDA-1088. 

The cost of Redemplo may vary depending on your location, the pharmacy you use, and your insurance coverage.

The manufacturer of Redemplo offers a support program called the Rely on Redemplo Patient Support Program. This program offers a copay card to help lower the cost of Redemplo.

The following describes safety considerations before starting Redemplo.

The manufacturer does not provide information about interactions with Redemplo. That doesn’t mean that interactions won’t exist in the future.

Quick tip: Create a medication list that includes all prescription drugs, over-the-counter drugs, and supplements to share with your doctor and pharmacist. This can help prevent interactions.

It may not be safe to receive Redemplo if you have certain health conditions.

Be sure to talk with your doctor about all of your health conditions before starting treatment with Redemplo.

Redemplo is a prescription injection used to help lower triglyceride levels in certain adults. It is used in combination with a low fat diet.

If you have questions about whether Redemplo is right for you, talk with your doctor.

title: How can a person's body change during pregnancy?
content:
Physical changes during pregnancy can affect more than just a person’s size and shape. People may experience changes in their skin, dental health, digestion, urinary function, and more.

Pregnancy brings about some obvious changes to the body, including a baby bump and increased weight. But, people may not realize that hormonal fluctuations during pregnancy can lead to changes in almost every part of the body.

This article discusses 10 changes that people may experience during pregnancy and tips on how to manage if these changes lead to unexpected emotional challenges. 

One of the most expected changes during pregnancy is an increase in size. In a person’s first pregnancy, a baby bump may not be obvious until the second trimester, but it can show earlier in subsequent pregnancies.

Factors such as a person’s body mass index (BMI) may influence how much weight doctors expect them to gain during pregnancy. However, various factors can contribute to weight increases, including:

Weight alone cannot indicate the health or growth of a baby during pregnancy. If anyone has any concerns about their weight or their baby’s growth, it is a good idea to speak with their healthcare team. 

During pregnancy, the breasts change to prepare for lactation. Estrogen increases in the early stages of pregnancy trigger the ductal system in the breast to expand and branch out.

The ductal system is the network of tubes in the breast that will transport milk from the lobules to the nipple during breastfeeding. 

Other changes that people may experience in the breast include:

Hormonal changes and increased blood volume can make pregnant people feel warmer, which may lead to increased sweating. 

It is important to drink enough water to stay hydrated if someone is experiencing increased levels of sweating. Other tips that may help include:

Hormonal changes during pregnancy can lead to blood vessel growth, increased inflammatory responses, and changes in a person’s oral microbiome and saliva. These changes can worsen or trigger dental issues. 

Common dental problems that occur during pregnancy include:

Although many skin changes that can occur during pregnancy are harmless, they can significantly impact a person’s self-esteem and mental health. Some may also indicate underlying issues that need treating.

Skin changes that people may experience during pregnancy include:

If someone notices changes to the skin, particularly new or changing moles or spots, it is important to check in with the healthcare team for a diagnosis, as serious dermatological issues such as skin cancer can occur during pregnancy.

Changes in the body during pregnancy, including increased pressure on the bladder and changes in the pelvic floor muscles, can affect a person’s lower urinary tract.

Examples of urinary changes people may notice during pregnancy include:

Pregnancy and childbirth may also increase the risk of urinary issues, such as incontinence, after pregnancy.

Weight increases, nutritional deficiencies, and loosening ligaments can all lead to musculoskeletal changes, including joint pain, during pregnancy. Areas in which people might experience pain include:

Performing light-to-moderate exercise throughout a pregnancy can help to preserve musculoskeletal health. Some people may benefit from adjusting their exercise routine to allow for less intense exercise or to avoid contact sports.

Gastrointestinal and digestive issues are very common in pregnancy and may result from increased pressure on the digestive system. Examples of potential issues include:

Gradual swelling affects many pregnant people and is often not a cause for concern, although it can be uncomfortable. Swelling may be most likely to affect the:

Some people may also notice swelling in their face or nose toward the end of pregnancy, which can contribute to changes in self-esteem. 

Swelling in pregnancy occurs because the body retains more fluids than usual. People may notice that swelling is most severe at the end of the day, in hot weather, or after spending a lot of time standing still.

Although gradual swelling is usually not cause for concern, sudden swelling can indicate a severe health issue, such as preeclampsia or a blood clot. 

If people experience sudden swelling anywhere on the body or swelling that only affects one part of the body, such as one leg, it is vital to seek immediate medical help.

Varicose veins are common during pregnancy. They usually affect the legs but can occur anywhere on the body. 

Varicose veins may look swollen or bumpy. They may also occur alongside other symptoms, including:

People may be able to relieve varicose veins by taking breaks when standing or sitting for long periods and working with their healthcare team to maintain a suitable weight throughout pregnancy.

Whether or not someone expects and understands how their body will change during pregnancy, the reality of those changes can be a shock and can affect a person’s mental health. 

Anyone experiencing problems with self-esteem or mental health can speak with their healthcare team. 

A doctor may be able to suggest treatments to relieve some physical changes during pregnancy, such as stool softeners to help with constipation. 

In other cases, they may be able to recommend support groups or mental health professionals who can help someone come to terms with the changes their body is going through.

“I wasn’t prepared for how my body would look and feel after giving birth the first time. During the last months of pregnancy, my belly was stretched tight, and it was quite a shock to go from that to a tummy of Jell-O.

“I tried to do a crunch a few weeks after the birth and couldn’t raise more than my head off the floor, which, looking back, wasn’t surprising, but at the time felt really shocking, having had fairly strong abs before pregnancy.

“My advice would be to be gentle with yourself and expect very little of your body during those first months postpartum. It’s called the fourth trimester for a reason! Gentle yoga and pilates will help build strength and stretch you out, and lay the foundations for slightly more intensive exercise later.”

— Ruth, Editor

Learn what to expect from postpartum recovery.

Quotes represent individual opinions. All content is strictly informational and should not be considered medical advice.

The amount of time it takes for physical changes due to pregnancy to reverse after pregnancy varies from person to person. In some cases, the changes will never entirely disappear, although they may become less noticeable with time.

The amount of time it takes for physical changes due to pregnancy to reverse after pregnancy varies from person to person. In some cases, the changes will never entirely disappear, although they may become less noticeable with time.

Some physical changes, such as stretch marks, are likely to remain after pregnancy, although these may fade and become less noticeable.

Some physical changes, such as stretch marks, are likely to remain after pregnancy, although these may fade and become less noticeable.

Hormonal and physiological changes in pregnancy can affect almost every part of a person’s body. In many cases, these changes are expected and not a cause for concern.

People can work with their healthcare team to manage symptoms if pregnancy changes are causing physical discomfort.

It is also important to seek help and support if changes during pregnancy begin to affect a person’s mental health.

title: When will Zepbound become more affordable?
content:
The out-of-pocket cost for Zepbound is expected to decrease for many people over time. While the official list price of the brand-name medication may not drop immediately, your actual monthly costs can drop through expanding insurance coverage, manufacturer savings programs, and increasing market competition.

If you want to save money on your prescription right now, you have a few actionable options. First, the drug’s manufacturer, Eli Lilly, offers a Zepbound Savings Card. This card works with or without insurance coverage, though savings are typically higher when used with a qualifying insurance plan.

If you have commercial insurance that covers the medication, this card can bring your out-of-pocket cost as low as $25 for a 3-month prescription. If you don’t use insurance or choose to self-pay, a third-party savings program may still help lower your out-of-pocket costs.

Additionally, throughout 2026, commercial insurance coverage for weight management medications has slowly expanded. Review your plan’s formulary to see whether Zepbound is a covered drug and find out what prior authorization steps you may need to take.

If you need help navigating this process, read our comprehensive guide on insurance coverage with Zepbound. 

An authorized generic version of Zepbound (tirzepatide) will not be available for several years. The manufacturer holds active patents that prevent other companies from producing a true generic until at least the 2030s. However, as new GLP-1 and GIP medications are developed and enter the market, this increased competition could eventually drive prices down across the entire class of weight management drugs.

A note on compounded tirzepatide: You may see “compounded tirzepatide” advertised online at much lower prices. Compounded formulas are not the same as generic Zepbound. They do not have the same rigorous testing, safety guarantees, or oversight from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) as the brand-name drug or future authorized generics.

To explore other safe, FDA-approved options that might fit your budget, read about weight loss drugs covered by insurance in 2026.

title: Can I skip a week of Wegovy?
content:
Skipping a week of Wegovy (semaglutide) is not recommended. Wegovy is designed to be used once weekly for the injection and once daily for the pill. This helps keep the drug at a steady level in the body, reduces the risk of side effects, and ensures it can work effectively.

However, life can sometimes get in the way of taking your medication at the right time, and you may occasionally miss a dose. 

If you’re considering spreading out doses of Wegovy to save money or reduce side effects, it’s not a good idea. Semaglutide, the active ingredient in Wegovy, has a long half-life of 1 week, meaning it stays in your system for 5 to 7 weeks after your last dose. Despite this, skipping doses on purpose can make the drug less effective for your long-term weight loss goals.

Important: Always use Wegovy as your doctor prescribes. Do not adjust your dosage unless your doctor advises it.

If you use the Wegovy injection, the rules for a missed dose depend on how much time is left before your next one. If your next scheduled dose is more than 2 days (48 hours) away, you should take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If your next dose is less than 2 days away, skip the missed dose and take your next dose on your regular day.

Never take two doses of Wegovy at the same time to make up for a missed dose. If you miss two or more weeks in a row, contact your healthcare professional before starting again. Long breaks may require you to restart at a lower dose to avoid severe stomach side effects, such as nausea or vomiting, while your body readjusts to the medication.

If you’re taking the Wegovy daily pill, skip any missed dose and resume your normal schedule the next morning.

To stay on track with your Wegovy schedule, try setting an alarm on your phone or using a medication reminder app. Always plan ahead by checking your supply and reordering your prescription early so you don’t run out of medication.

How can I stay on track with my weekly GLP-1 injections while traveling?

“It’s important to carry enough medication and supplies to last while you’re away, especially if you’re traveling longer than a week. It can be helpful to use an alarm or a reminder app on your phone to help keep you on track with your GLP-1 injections while traveling.”

— Kristina D. Carter, PharmD, MBA, MHA

Read more: How to Travel with Wegovy

title: How can makeup affect psoriasis?
content:
People with psoriasis can still wear makeup, although they may need to adjust the products they use to keep things gentle on the skin. 

In this article, learn how makeup can affect psoriasis symptoms, whether cosmetics can cover psoriasis, tips for applying makeup over psoriasis lesions, and more.

Skin care and cosmetic products can play a role in psoriasis treatment. For example, some research suggests that ingredients such as salicylic acid or ceramides may help soften psoriasis plaques and help the skin absorb topical treatments.

However, some people with psoriasis may experience skin sensitivity and worsening symptoms, including dryness and inflammation, when using cosmetics with certain ingredients. These ingredients may include:

Makeup may also affect the appearance of psoriasis, giving the impression that it is more or less severe. For some people, makeup can help cover or disguise psoriasis flare-ups. While others may feel that makeup emphasizes their symptoms.

Psoriasis symptoms can vary from person to person. Whether makeup is effective at covering psoriasis may depend on various factors, including:

Some people may find that psoriasis is effective at disguising their symptoms, while others may feel that makeup exaggerates their psoriasis lesions.

Since it can vary so much from person to person, it may help for people with psoriasis to try different products and application techniques to see what works for them.

However, it is important to note that people with visible skin conditions should not feel obligated to cover their flare-ups. 

Visible skin conditions like psoriasis can lead to low self-esteem and body dissatisfaction. Public stigma and self-stigma can contribute to this. However, psychological treatments and support can help people with psoriasis overcome stigma and come to terms with their condition.

Whether or not makeup effectively covers my psoriasis seems to depend on the specific symptoms I’m experiencing during a flare-up. If my symptoms are mild — a small amount of skin discoloration and itching or pain — applying makeup after my skin care can help disguise things.

However, if my symptoms are more severe, with flaking over large patches of skin, I personally feel that makeup draws attention to my psoriasis.

I find cosmetics most effective during flare-ups when I focus on addressing one symptom. For example, if my skin is flaky, I’ll prioritize proper moisturizing and hydration, whereas if my skin is calm but discolored, I’ll target patches with a color corrector.

Makeup can sit differently on regular skin than psoriasis patches, which can be noticeable. However, I know I pay more attention to this on myself than other people do. 

Whether I wear makeup or not, I feel my best when I’m confident in myself. If applying makeup over psoriasis helps someone feel more confident, I believe it’s a great idea. Experimenting with different products, application techniques, and styles can help people find the look that they prefer.

Meet your author: Megan Austwick

The following tips may help someone achieve a favorable application when using makeup over psoriasis:

Doctors may recommend various treatments for people with psoriasis, including:

Sticking to the treatment routine a doctor recommends can help clear or reduce psoriasis lesions, which may make it easier to apply makeup.

According to the National Psoriasis Foundation, moisturizing the skin well can help reduce psoriasis symptoms, including dryness and itchiness. 

Properly preparing and priming the skin before applying makeup may also help cosmetics stay on the skin for longer. 

The best priming routine may depend on a person’s skin type, so it may be helpful to experiment with different products to see what works best.

People may also notice that makeup sits differently on psoriasis patches than the rest of their skin, so a slightly different priming routine may be necessary on those areas.

Thick or high coverage skin products may be more likely to cling to dry patches of skin. 

People with psoriasis may find makeup easier to apply if they stick with lightweight products, start with a thin layer, and build up their application gradually.

There is no obligation to cover up psoriasis patches with makeup. In fact, some people may prefer instead to focus on another aspect of their makeup, where they do not have symptoms. 

For example, people can focus on cosmetics for skin that is unaffected by their symptoms. Alternatively, people could avoid the skin altogether and only apply cosmetics to the eyes or brows.

People with psoriasis may be more likely to experience shame, psychological stress, and mental health issues due to their symptoms. 

However, it is important to remember that psoriasis is a chronic health condition. There is no shame in having visible symptoms, and no obligation to cover or hide those symptoms.

Makeup can help people feel more confident and secure in their appearance. It is also a source of joy and expression for many. People who wear makeup may feel better when focusing on the beauty and art of the cosmetics, instead of using makeup as a mask to cover up.

There is no cure for psoriasis, but working with a healthcare professional can help people find effective treatments to manage their symptoms during a flare-up.

Research suggests that around 75% of people with psoriasis experience changes in daily activities and habits due to feelings of embarrassment, stigma, and low self-esteem.

If psoriasis interferes with daily activities, leads to social isolation, or causes mental health issues, it is important to reach out for help.

A dermatologist can prescribe and recommend treatments to help manage psoriasis symptoms, which may help improve a person’s self-esteem. 

They can also offer treatments and referrals to specialists, such as psychiatrists, who can help with mental health issues.

Psoriasis does not prevent anyone from wearing makeup. However, some ingredients in certain cosmetics, including alcohol and essential oils, may irritate the skin and worsen psoriasis symptoms.

Small adjustments to the products a person wears and their makeup application techniques can help makeup to sit effectively on skin with psoriasis flares.

It is important to seek help from a healthcare professional if psoriasis symptoms begin to affect a person’s self-esteem or mental health. Treatments are available to help manage a person’s symptoms and improve their mental health.
